description: Rework the draft mechanic: deal 8 cards (2xT3, 3xT2, 3xT1) instead of 7. Players pick 5 with a tier constraint: max 1 T3 and max 2 T2 in the final hand. Players get to choose which T3 to keep. AI auto-draft updated to respect tier constraints.

userStories.title: Update deal constants and tier-slotted generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deals to contain exactly 2 T3, 3 T2, and 3 T1 cards (8 total) -- so players get a meaningful T3 choice and better composition.
acceptanceCriteria: Change `DEAL_SIZE = 8` (from 7) in `app/rules/deck.py`
Remove `MAX_PER_TIER = 3` constant (replaced by exact tier slots)
Add `DEAL_TIER_SLOTS: dict[int, int] = {3: 2, 2: 3, 1: 3}` constant

Rewrite `generate_matched_deals()` to build deals by filling exact tier slots: for each tier, draw from the shuffled pool respecting rarity/copy/named constraints until slots are filled, alternating between deals for balance
Cost tolerance check still applies after generation
Update `validate_deal()` to check exact tier slot counts instead of MAX_PER_TIER
Update `_can_add_to_deal()` to enforce tier slot limits instead of MAX_PER_TIER
Update all tests in `tests/test_deck.py` that reference DEAL_SIZE=7 or MAX_PER_TIER
Add test: generated deals have exactly {3:2, 2:3, 1:3} tier distribution
Add test: both deals in a matched pair have correct tier distribution
`uv run pytest` passes
`uv run pyright` passes

userStories.title: Hand tier constraints: max 1 T3, max 2 T2 in selected hand
userStories.description: As a player, I want my final hand of 5 to be constrained to at most 1 T3 and 2 T2 cards -- so hand composition is balanced and T3 choice matters.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `HAND_TIER_LIMITS: dict[int, int] = {3: 1, 2: 2}` constant in `app/rules/deck.py` (T1 is unconstrained)
Add `validate_hand_tiers(selected_keys: list[str], card_lookup: dict[str, CardDefinition]) -> list[str]` function that returns error strings if tier limits are violated
In `game_service.submit_draft()`: after existing validation, call `validate_hand_tiers` and raise ValueError if any errors
Add test: selecting 2 T3 cards raises validation error
Add test: selecting 3 T2 cards raises validation error
Add test: selecting 1 T3 + 2 T2 + 2 T1 passes validation
Add test: selecting 0 T3 + 2 T2 + 3 T1 passes validation
`uv run pytest` passes
`uv run pyright` passes

userStories.title: AI auto-draft respects tier constraints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the AI auto-draft to respect the new hand tier limits -- so AI opponents build legal hands.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `_ai_auto_draft()` in `game_service.py` to respect `HAND_TIER_LIMITS`
Easy: random sample of 5 from deal, but re-sample if tier limits violated (retry up to 50 times, then fall back to greedy valid selection)
Medium: pick top 5 by total side sum, but enforce tier limits (if best 5 violate limits, swap out excess high-tier cards for next-best lower-tier)
Hard/Nightmare: pick top 5 by coverage score, same tier-limit enforcement
Add test: AI draft never produces a hand with 2+ T3 cards
Add test: AI draft never produces a hand with 3+ T2 cards
`uv run pytest` passes
`uv run pyright` passes

userStories.title: Frontend: update draft UI for 8-card deal with tier constraints
userStories.description: As a player, I want the draft screen to show 8 cards and enforce the tier limits visually -- so I understand the constraints while picking.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `HAND_SIZE` reference in `DraftingGameView.tsx` (still 5, but deal now shows 8 cards)
Show tier constraint info: 'Pick 5 cards (max 1 Tier 3, max 2 Tier 2)' in the draft instructions
When player has already selected 1 T3 card, visually disable remaining T3 cards (opacity + cannot select)
When player has already selected 2 T2 cards, visually disable remaining T2 cards
Show tier badge on each card in the draft view (T1/T2/T3 label or color indicator)
Update draft confirmation text to mention tier constraints if selection is invalid
Update frontend tests in `web/src/__tests__/drafting.test.tsx` for 8-card deal and tier constraints
Add test: T3 cards disabled after selecting 1 T3
Add test: T2 cards disabled after selecting 2 T2
`cd web && bun run test --run` passes

userStories.title: Documentation refresh: update deal/draft rules in all docs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all documentation to reflect the new 8-card deal and tier-constrained hand -- so docs stay accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `CLAUDE.md`: Deal & Draft section to say 'dealt 8 cards (2xT3, 3xT2, 3xT1), picks 5 to keep (max 1 T3, max 2 T2)'
Update `docs/GAME_RULES_OVERVIEW.md` match setup section
No stale references to '7-card deal' or 'draw 7' remain in any doc
